theme: Nova payment processing outage on billing renewal day plus Vertex Series A Sequoia call outcome and Orion Sprint 19 kickoff

events.text: CRITICAL: Stripe webhook handler throwing 500s — payment processing completely down since 18:54, renewal billing day for 340 enterprise accounts
NOVA-1301 Critical — Stripe webhook handler down since 18:54, 340 enterprise accounts on renewal billing cycle today
Infra: Stripe webhook endpoint returning 500 — handler deployed at 18:50 with new signature validation logic
REVERT: stripe-webhook-handler.py — signature validation update breaking all incoming Stripe events
Engineering: revert deployed at 19:01 — testing Stripe webhook receipt now
Stripe webhook test event received and processed — handler responding correctly after revert
NOVA-1302 — revert confirmed working, webhook handler operational, 7-minute outage window
DBA: checking which of 340 renewal accounts were affected — billing events queued during 18:54-19:01 window
test: audit billing events queued 18:54-19:01 — identify renewals that need reprocessing
DBA: 47 renewal events failed during the 7-minute window — 47 accounts not yet billed for today's renewal
NOVA-1303 — 47 renewal billing events failed, need reprocessing
Stripe: failed webhooks will auto-retry at 19:10, 19:40, 20:40 — monitoring to confirm all 47 process on first retry
NOVA-1304 — Stripe auto-retry monitoring: next retry 19:10, watching 47 events
PM: none of the 47 affected accounts will notice — billing happens server-side, no customer-facing error shown
Subject: Stripe webhook outage — internal | Body: 7-minute outage 18:54-19:01, 47 renewals queued for retry at 19:10, no customer-visible impact
Sequoia 15:30 call completed — CEO and CFO debriefing team, results positive
VTX-1001 — Sequoia call debrief, CEO and CFO reporting outcomes
CEO: Rohan (Sequoia) had 3 financial model questions — burn rate normalisation, CAC payback period, and Series A use-of-funds breakdown
CFO: all 3 questions answered confidently — burn rate $185K/month, CAC payback 11 months, use-of-funds: 60% engineering, 25% sales, 15% ops
VTX-1002 — Sequoia satisfied with all 3 financial answers, requesting partner committee review next week
CEO: Rohan requesting partner committee presentation next Thursday — Sequoia moving to next stage

Stripe retry: 47 of 47 events processed successfully — all 340 renewal accounts now correctly billed
NOVA-1303 closed — 47 renewals reprocessed successfully at 19:10, all 340 accounts billed
Billing day complete — 340 enterprise renewals processed, total $284,000 in renewal revenue, zero customer impact

NOVA-1301 resolved — 7-minute outage, zero customer impact, all 340 accounts billed

Root cause investigation: new Stripe signature validation logic used wrong HMAC algorithm — SHA-256 vs SHA-512 mismatch
NOVA-1305 root cause — HMAC algorithm mismatch: SHA-256 in code vs SHA-512 in Stripe docs
Engineering: fixing signature validation with correct algorithm before re-deploying
fix: stripe-webhook-handler.py — use SHA-256 HMAC matching Stripe webhook signature spec
NOVA-1306 fix in progress — correct HMAC algorithm implementation

test: Stripe webhook signature validation — 10 Stripe test events, all signatures verified correctly
